summary: Ancient Greek comic playwright, the master of Old Comedy. Known for The Clouds, Lysistrata, The Frogs, The Birds, The Wasps, Thesmophoriazusae, The Acharnians, Peace, Ecclesiazusae (Assemblywomen), and Plutus. Frequently satirized Athenian politics, Socrates, and Euripides. Works performed at the City Dionysia.

works.name: The Clouds
works.description: Comedy satirizing Socrates and intellectual culture through the Thinkery, a school run by Socrates.

clues.clue: Strepsiades sends his son Pheidippides to the Thinkery to learn how to avoid paying debts from buying horses
examples: Strepsiades tries to get out of his debts by enrolling his son Pheidippides in Socrates's 'Thinkery'
Pheidippides beats up his father Strepsiades after learning sophism from Socrates at the Thinkery

clues.clue: Play ends with Strepsiades and slave Xanthias burning down the Thinkery
examples: ends the play by burning down the Thinkery with his slave Xanthias
the protagonist avoids paying his debts when he and Xanthias burn down the Thinkery

clues.clue: Right Logic (Superior Argument) vs. Wrong Logic (Inferior Argument) debate — worse argument wins
examples: summons the personifications of a better argument and a worse argument, who argue with each other until the worse argument wins
critiqued intellectuals through an agon, or debate, between Superior and Inferior Arguments

clues.clue: Socrates sits in a basket floating in the air, says he doesn't want his mind to dry out like lettuce
examples: When asked why he is sitting in a basket floating in the air, this man says that he doesn't want his mind to dry out like a piece of lettuce

clues.clue: A lizard defecates on Socrates while he contemplates the moon
examples: one of these animals defecated on Socrates while the philosopher was busy contemplating the moon's path
A scholar from this city who was once defecated on by a lizard

clues.clue: Strepsiades dismissed after Socrates finds him masturbating under a blanket instead of meditating
examples: dismissed as a student after Socrates finds him masturbating under a blanket instead of meditating

works.name: Lysistrata
works.description: Comedy in which Athenian women withhold sex to end the Peloponnesian War.

clues.clue: Women withhold sex from husbands to end the Peloponnesian War
examples: convinces Athenian women to withhold sex from their husbands in an attempt to end the Peloponnesian War
a group of women attempts to stop the Peloponnesian War

clues.clue: Women swear an oath on a skin of Thasian wine not to 'crouch down like a lioness on all fours' / 'take the pose of a lioness on a cheese grater'
examples: characters in a play by this man swear an oath using a skin of Thasian wine instead of sacrificing a sheep
vow not to 'take the pose of a lioness on a cheese grater' while swearing an oath on a wine skin

clues.clue: Lysistrata pours water on a magistrate who scorns her wool-working analogy for society
examples: pours a bucket of water over the head of a magistrate who scorns her for analogizing the ideal workings of society to the woolworking

clues.clue: A magistrate confuses a herald's erection for a hidden lance
examples: A play by this man includes a scene in which a magistrate confuses a herald's erection for a hidden lance

clues.clue: Characters include Caloniki and Lampito
examples: characters including Caloniki and Lampito vow not to 'take the pose of a lioness on a cheese grater'

works.name: The Frogs
works.description: Comedy in which Dionysus travels to the underworld to bring back a dead playwright.

clues.clue: Dionysus travels to the underworld to bring back Euripides (but ends up bringing Aeschylus instead)
examples: Dionysus goes to the underworld to bring back Euripides in his play The Frogs
bringing Aeschylus back from the dead

clues.clue: Dionysus trades clothes with slave Xanthias to avoid being mistaken for Heracles
examples: Dionysus trades clothes with his slave Xanthias before bringing Aeschylus back from the dead

clues.clue: Chorus of frogs chants 'brekekek koax koax'
examples: Frogs who go 'brekekek koax koax'
Dionysus engages in a comedic debate with this play's chanting chorus

clues.clue: Quote about Alcibiades: 'Twere best to rear no lion in the state'
examples: a playwright claims that ''Twere best to rear no lion in the state: But having reared, 'tis best to humour him'

clues.clue: 1974 Yale musical adaptation used the varsity swim team as the chorus, water splashed out of pool
examples: adapted into a disastrous 1974 musical in New Haven, where the title croaking chorus of his The Frogs was played by the Yale varsity swim team

works.name: The Birds
works.description: Comedy in which Pisthetaerus and Euelpides create the city of Cloudcuckooland to block the gods from receiving offerings.

clues.clue: Pisthetaerus creates Cloudcuckooland to blockade the Olympian gods from receiving human offerings
examples: Pisthetaerus takes over the city of Cloudcuckooland and blocks the offerings of the people below to the gods
The goal of creating this location is to blockade the Olympian gods from reaching Earth

clues.clue: Prometheus visits secretly with a parasol so Zeus cannot see him
examples: Prometheus visits this city secretly
He wields a parasol so Zeus cannot see him

clues.clue: Iris mistreated; she flies away and reports to Zeus
examples: Iris is the subject of many raunchy jokes during her trip to this city
Iris flies away and reports her mistreatment to Zeus

clues.clue: Heracles tricked into a treaty after being told he's a bastard who can't inherit Zeus's estate
examples: this city's ruler convinces Heracles to agree to a treaty after convincing him that, as a bastard, he cannot inherit Zeus's estate

clues.clue: Meton the geometer chased away
examples: one character chases away the geometer Meton

clues.clue: Chorus threatens to defecate on the judges if the play doesn't win
examples: the chorus threatens to shit on the judges if the play doesn't win the City Dionysia

works.name: The Wasps
works.description: Comedy about Philocleon, who is addicted to serving on juries, and his son Anticleon who tries to stop him.

clues.clue: Philocleon is addicted to jury service; son Anticleon tries to cure him; mock trial of a dog for stealing cheese
examples: a son who hates Cleon sets up a mock court for his father, who loves Cleon, and is addicted to serving on juries
a mock-trial wherein a household dog accuses another of stealing cheese

clues.clue: Criticizes the politician Cleon
examples: Aristophanes criticizes the politician Cleon both in The Clouds and in this later play

works.name: Thesmophoriazusae
works.description: Comedy set at the Thesmophoria festival, where women discuss Euripides's unflattering portrayals of them.

clues.clue: Euripides uses disguises to rescue a friend captured at the Thesmophoria festival; women including Micca discuss Euripides's portrayal of women
examples: Euripides uses various disguises to try to save his friend after he is captured at the title festival
a man threatens what he believes to be a baby, only to learn that it is just a wine skin

clues.clue: Contains the first documented use of the word 'mimesis'
examples: This play contains the first documented use of the word mimesis

clues.clue: A man cross-dresses to infiltrate the festival to spy on what women think of him
examples: A character cross-dresses to infiltrate this event so that he can spy on a group of women

works.name: Peace
works.description: Comedy in which Trygaeus rides a giant dung beetle to free the goddess Peace.

clues.clue: Trygaeus flies to an audience with the gods on a giant dung beetle
examples: one character flies to an audience with the gods on the back of a giant dung beetle

clues.clue: Festival and Harvest are companions of Peace; Trygaeus marries Harvest
examples: Festival and Harvest are companions of the title character

works.name: Other Works and General
works.description: Ecclesiazusae (Assemblywomen), Plutus, The Acharnians, The Knights, and general facts about Old Comedy.

clues.clue: Ecclesiazusae: Praxagora leads women to disguise as men and vote themselves into power; impose communal property and sexual rules
examples: Praxagora gets the women of Athens to disguise themselves as men and vote themselves into power so that they can impose glorious socialism

clues.clue: The Acharnians: Dicaeopolis tries to secure peace with Sparta; Euripides asked for vegetables and a beggar's costume
examples: this play about Dicaeopolis' attempt to secure peace with Sparta

clues.clue: The Knights: Agoracritus (sausage seller) vs. Cleon the Paphlagonian for Demos's favor; oracle says sausage seller will rule Athens
examples: Agoracritus and Cleon the Paphlagonian get into a shouting contest

clues.clue: Plutus: slave Cario impersonates a snake in Temple of Asclepius; Chremylus visits Delphic Oracle
examples: the slave Cario reminisces about impersonating a snake in the Temple of Asclepius

clues.clue: Master of Old Comedy; parabasis is the section where the chorus addresses the audience
examples: This Athenian playwright was the most famous writer of Old Comedy
the paragon of this genre, in which obscenity, mythological characters, and political topics were common

clues.clue: Won first place for comedy at the City Dionysia with The Babylonians in 426 BC
examples: In 426 BC, Aristophanes won first place for comedy with his play The Babylonians at this Athenian festival

comprehensive_summary:
Aristophanes was the foremost playwright of Athenian Old Comedy, a genre characterized by obscenity, mythological characters, political satire, and a six-part structure including the parodos, agon, and parabasis (where the chorus directly addresses the audience). He won first place for comedy at the City Dionysia in 426 BC with The Babylonians.

The Clouds satirizes Socrates and intellectualism. Strepsiades, drowning in debt from his son Pheidippides's horse purchases, enrolls at Socrates's school, the Thinkery. Socrates sits in a basket floating in the air to keep his mind from drying out like lettuce and was once defecated on by a lizard while contemplating the moon. A debate between Right Logic (Superior Argument) and Wrong Logic (Inferior Argument) results in the worse argument winning. The play ends when Strepsiades and his slave Xanthias burn down the Thinkery.

Lysistrata follows women who withhold sex from their husbands to end the Peloponnesian War. The women swear an oath on a skin of Thasian wine, vowing not to 'crouch like a lioness on all fours.' Characters include Caloniki and the Spartan Lampito. Lysistrata pours water on a magistrate who scorns her woolworking analogy for society.

The Frogs follows Dionysus traveling to the underworld to bring back Euripides but ultimately choosing Aeschylus instead. Dionysus swaps clothes with his slave Xanthias to avoid being mistaken for Heracles. The chorus of frogs chants 'brekekek koax koax.' A 1974 Yale musical adaptation used the varsity swim team as the frog chorus.

The Birds features Pisthetaerus and Euelpides creating Cloudcuckooland to blockade the gods from human offerings. Prometheus sneaks in under a parasol so Zeus can't see him. Iris is mistreated; Heracles is tricked into a treaty. The geometer Meton is chased away.

The Wasps centers on Philocleon's jury addiction and his son Anticleon's efforts to cure him, culminating in a mock trial where a household dog is accused of stealing cheese. Thesmophoriazusae is set at the Thesmophoria festival where women discuss Euripides's unflattering portrayals; it contains the first documented use of the word 'mimesis.' Peace has Trygaeus riding a giant dung beetle to rescue the goddess Peace. Ecclesiazusae (Assemblywomen) features Praxagora leading women to disguise as men, vote themselves into power, and impose communal property. The Knights pits the sausage-seller Agoracritus against Cleon for the favor of Demos.
